actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Builds and configures applications on Hyperlane, uses SDK and CLI, configures hooks and ISMs, and deploys applications. (Docs 4, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 30, 31, 39, 45)
capabilities: Use Hyperlane to share state between blockchains (Doc 4)

actors.name: User
actors.description: Interacts with the Hyperlane Protocol and applications built on it, such as initiating token transfers. (Docs 3, 21)
capabilities: Initiate remote token transfers via TokenRouter (Doc 21)
Use Hyperlane CLI to send messages or deploy routes (Doc 31)

actors.name: Relayer
actors.description: Off-chain agent responsible for relaying messages from an origin blockchain to a destination blockchain. (Docs 10, 21, 29)
capabilities: Read messages from origin chain Mailbox (Doc 10, 21)
Submit 'process' transactions to destination chain Mailbox (Doc 10, 21)
Make POST requests to CCIP-read server (Doc 29)

actors.name: Validator
actors.description: Off-chain agent responsible for observing messages on an origin chain and submitting attestations to the destination chain's Interchain Security Module. (Docs 10, 13, 19)
capabilities: Run as a node on AWS or similar infrastructure (Doc 13)

actors.name: Scraper Agent
actors.description: Off-chain agent that collects and stores on-chain data from Hyperlane contracts into a database. (Docs 10, 11)
capabilities: Read data from Hyperlane contracts on multiple chains (Doc 11)
Write data to a Postgres database (Doc 11)

actors.name: Deployer
actors.description: An account responsible for deploying Hyperlane smart contracts, Interchain Accounts, or related infrastructure. (Docs 10, 20, 21, 31, 43)
capabilities: Deploy infrastructure components (e.g., using Terraform, Helm) (Doc 13, 43)

actors.name: Github API
actors.description: External service providing access to Hyperlane source code, contract artifacts, and registry information. (Docs 24, 37, 45)

actors.name: GCP Service Account
actors.description: A Google Cloud Platform service account with permissions to access secrets in GCP Secret Manager. (Doc 43)
capabilities: Access secrets from GCP Secret Manager (roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor) (Doc 43)

actors.name: CI System
actors.description: Continuous Integration system used for building and testing Hyperlane components, including Docker images for agents. (Doc 4, 10)

assets.name: Interchain Message
assets.description: Arbitrary data packet sent from one blockchain to another via Hyperlane. (Doc 4, 21)

assets.name: Fungible Token (Native/ERC20)
assets.description: Standard fungible tokens (e.g., ETH, ERC20) transferred across chains. (Doc 21)

assets.name: Non-Fungible Token (ERC721)
assets.description: Standard non-fungible tokens transferred across chains. (Doc 21)

assets.name: Synthetic Token
assets.description: A representation of a token on a non-canonical chain, minted by a TokenRouter. (Doc 21)

assets.name: Yield-Bearing Asset
assets.description: An underlying asset that accrues yield, often managed by an ERC4626 vault. (Doc 22)

assets.name: Message Metadata
assets.description: Data accompanying an interchain message, used by Hooks and ISMs for custom logic and security. (Doc 18, 19)

assets.name: Transaction Fees
assets.description: Value paid to cover costs of on-chain operations, including message dispatch and relaying. (Doc 18, 21)

assets.name: Contract Configuration Data
assets.description: Parameters for smart contracts, such as ISM addresses for recipients or hook configurations for dispatchers. (Doc 18, 19, 45)

assets.name: Agent Configuration File
assets.description: Configuration files used by off-chain agents (Relayer, Validator, Scraper) defining their operational parameters. (Doc 10)

assets.name: Smart Contract Artifacts
assets.description: Compiled bytecode and ABI for smart contracts (e.g., EVM Typechain, Cairo ABI). (Doc 17, 24)

assets.name: CCIP-read Request Data
assets.description: ABI-encoded data sent to the CCIP-read server by a Relayer for off-chain data retrieval. (Doc 29)

assets.name: Github API Key
assets.description: Authentication key for accessing the Github API with higher rate limits, used by Github Proxy. (Doc 37)

assets.name: GCP Service Account Key
assets.description: Credentials for a GCP service account, used to access GCP resources like Secret Manager. (Doc 10, 43)

assets.name: GCP Secret
assets.description: Sensitive data (e.g., API keys, credentials) stored in GCP Secret Manager. (Doc 43)

assets.name: Scraper Database Content
assets.description: Data collected by the Scraper agent from Hyperlane contracts, stored in a Postgres database. (Doc 11)

assets.name: Validator Attestation
assets.description: A signed statement from a Validator confirming the validity of an interchain message. (Doc 10, 19)

assets.name: TokenMessage Payload
assets.description: Encoded data within an interchain message specific to token transfers, containing amount and recipient. (Doc 21)

assets.name: ERC4626 Shares
assets.description: Tokens representing a user's share in an ERC4626 yield-bearing vault. (Doc 22)

assets.name: Relayer Private Key
assets.description: Private key used by the Relayer agent for signing transactions on destination chains.

assets.name: Validator Private Key
assets.description: Private key used by the Validator agent for signing attestations.

assets.name: Hyperlane Source Code
assets.description: The source code of the Hyperlane protocol and associated tooling, typically hosted on Github. (Doc 4)

components.name: Mailbox Contract
components.description: Core Hyperlane smart contract on each chain responsible for dispatching outgoing messages and processing incoming messages. (Doc 17, 18, 19, 21)

components.name: RequiredHook Contract
components.description: A type of Post-Dispatch Hook that is mandatorily executed by the Mailbox for certain messages. (Doc 18)

components.name: DefaultHook Contract
components.description: A type of Post-Dispatch Hook that is executed by the Mailbox if no specific hook is provided or after a required hook. (Doc 18)

components.name: Custom PostDispatchHook Contract
components.description: A developer-defined smart contract that implements IPostDispatchHook for custom logic on the origin chain after message dispatch. (Doc 18)

components.name: Custom ISM Contract
components.description: A developer-defined smart contract that implements IInterchainSecurityModule for custom message verification logic on the destination chain. (Doc 19)

components.name: TokenRouter Contract
components.description: Smart contract (Native, Collateral, or Synthetic variants) that facilitates cross-chain token transfers (Warp Routes). (Doc 21)

components.name: HypERC4626Collateral Contract
components.description: A TokenRouter extension for warping ERC4626-compatible yield-bearing assets from their canonical chain. (Doc 22)

components.name: HypERC4626 Contract (Synthetic Yield)
components.description: A TokenRouter extension representing warped ERC4626 assets on a non-canonical chain, handling rebasing. (Doc 22)

components.name: ERC4626 Vault Contract
components.description: A standard ERC4626 smart contract vault holding yield-bearing assets. (Doc 22)

components.name: Relayer Off-chain Service
components.description: Off-chain bot that monitors Mailbox contracts for dispatched messages and submits them to the destination chain's Mailbox. (Doc 10, 21)

components.name: Validator Off-chain Service
components.description: Off-chain bot that monitors Mailbox contracts and provides attestations for messages to ISMs. (Doc 10, 13)

components.name: Scraper Off-chain Service
components.description: Off-chain bot that reads Hyperlane contract data from blockchains and stores it in a database. (Doc 10, 11)

components.name: Postgres Database
components.description: SQL database used by the Scraper service to store indexed blockchain data. (Doc 11)

components.name: CCIP-read Server
components.description: Off-chain service that handles CCIP-read requests, often for providing proofs or off-chain data to smart contracts. (Doc 29)

components.name: Github Proxy Service
components.description: A Cloudflare Worker that proxies requests to the Github API using an authenticated API key. (Doc 37)

components.name: GCP Secret Manager
components.description: External Google Cloud Platform service for storing and managing secrets. (Doc 43)

components.name: HelloWorld Router Contract
components.description: An example application smart contract that can dispatch and receive Hyperlane messages. (Doc 39)

components.name: InterchainAccount (ICA) Contract
components.description: A smart contract on a remote chain controlled by an account on an origin chain, enabling cross-chain interactions. (Doc 20)

components.name: Generic ERC20 Contract
components.description: A standard ERC20 token contract, external to Hyperlane core but interacted with by Warp Routes. (Doc 21)

components.name: Multisig ISM Contract
components.description: An Interchain Security Module that requires a threshold of validator signatures to verify a message. (Doc 16, 44, 45)

components.name: Pausable ISM Contract
components.description: An Interchain Security Module that allows an owner to pause message verification. (Doc 44)

components.name: DefaultFallbackRouting ISM Contract
components.description: An Interchain Security Module that routes messages to different ISMs based on configured rules, with a default fallback. (Doc 30, 44)

components.name: Hyperlane Registry
components.description: Centralized or distributed source of Hyperlane configuration, contract addresses, and chain metadata. (Doc 24, 30, 31, 42, 45)

components.name: Interchain Query System (IQS)
components.description: System for generalizing view calls to contracts on remote chains, using a transparent multicall API. (Doc 20)

data_flows.data: Token transfer request (destination, recipient, amount, value for native tokens)
data_flows.description: User initiates a remote token transfer by calling 'transferRemote' on a TokenRouter contract (e.g., Native ETH transfer). (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Interchain Message (TokenMessage payload: recipient, amount) and dispatch parameters (destination domain). Asset: 201, 217.
data_flows.description: TokenRouter (origin chain) calls 'dispatch' on the Mailbox contract to send the token transfer message. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Dispatched Interchain Message content and metadata. Asset: 201, 206.
data_flows.description: Relayer Service monitors/indexes the Mailbox (origin chain) for dispatched messages. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Interchain Message and proof/attestation. Asset: 201, 216 (implicitly via ISM).
data_flows.description: Relayer Service calls 'process' on the Mailbox contract (destination chain) to deliver the message. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Decoded message payload (recipient, amount). Asset: 217.
data_flows.description: Mailbox (destination chain) calls 'handle' on the TokenRouter contract (destination chain). (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Tokens (minted synthetic or released collateral). Asset: 202, 204.
data_flows.description: TokenRouter (destination chain) mints synthetic tokens or transfers collateral tokens to the final recipient. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Dispatch parameters (destination, recipient, body, metadata, hook address, value). Asset: 201, 206, 207.
data_flows.description: Developer (or application) calls 'dispatch' on Mailbox, specifying a PostDispatchHook. (Doc 18)

data_flows.data: Message content, metadata, and value. Asset: 201, 206, 207.
data_flows.description: Mailbox calls 'postDispatch' on the specified Custom PostDispatchHook contract. (Doc 18)

data_flows.data: Message metadata and content for verification. Asset: 201, 206.
data_flows.description: Mailbox (destination chain) calls 'verify' on the recipient's configured Custom ISM. (Doc 19)

data_flows.data: Message origin, sender, and body. Asset: 201.
data_flows.description: Mailbox (destination chain) calls 'handle' on the recipient application contract (e.g., HelloWorld Router) after successful ISM verification. (Doc 19, 39)

data_flows.data: Yield asset transfer request (destination, recipient, amount). Asset: 205, 217.
data_flows.description: User initiates a remote transfer of yield-bearing assets via HypERC4626Collateral contract. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: ERC20 transferFrom call (User, HypERC4626Collateral, amount). Asset: 202 (underlying collateral).
data_flows.description: HypERC4626Collateral pulls the underlying ERC20 collateral from the user. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: Deposit call (amount, HypERC4626Collateral). Asset: 205.
data_flows.description: HypERC4626Collateral deposits the collateral into the ERC4626 Vault. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: ERC4626 shares. Asset: 218.
data_flows.description: ERC4626 Vault returns shares to HypERC4626Collateral. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: Interchain Message (payload: recipient, shares, exchangeRate, nonce). Asset: 201, 217, 218.
data_flows.description: HypERC4626Collateral dispatches a message via Mailbox containing share information. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: Message payload (recipient, shares, exchangeRate, nonce). Asset: 217, 218.
data_flows.description: Mailbox (destination chain) delivers message to HypERC4626 (Synthetic Yield) contract, which mints synthetic shares. (Doc 22)

data_flows.data: POST Request with ABI-encoded data and sender address. Asset: 211.
data_flows.description: Relayer makes a POST request to the CCIP-read Server for off-chain data retrieval. (Doc 29)

data_flows.data: Authenticated Github API request. Asset: 212.
data_flows.description: Github Proxy Service makes an authenticated request to the Github API to fetch artifacts. (Doc 37)

data_flows.data: Indexed blockchain data. Asset: 215.
data_flows.description: Scraper Off-chain Service writes collected on-chain data to the Postgres Database. (Doc 11)

data_flows.data: Approval call (spender: TokenRouter, amount: infinity).
data_flows.description: User (Alice) approves the CollateralTokenRouter to spend her ERC20 tokens. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: ERC20 tokens (amount). Asset: 202.
data_flows.description: ERC20 contract transfers tokens from User (Alice) to the CollateralTokenRouter upon 'transferFrom' call. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Burn synthetic token event. Asset: 204.
data_flows.description: SyntheticTokenRouter (origin) burns user's (Alice's) synthetic tokens when transferring back to canonical chain. (Doc 21)

data_flows.data: Query request (target chain, contract, call data)
data_flows.description: User/Developer sends a query request to the Interchain Query System. (Doc 20)

data_flows.data: Interchain Message (query payload). Asset: 201.
data_flows.description: Interchain Query System dispatches the query as an interchain message via Mailbox to the target chain. (Doc 20)

data_flows.data: Configuration request
data_flows.description: Developer/Agent requests configuration data from Hyperlane Registry. (Doc 30, 45)

data_flows.data: Contract Configuration Data, Smart Contract Artifacts. Asset: 208, 210.
data_flows.description: Hyperlane Registry provides configuration data to Developer/Agent. (Doc 30, 45)

data_flows.data: Contract artifacts, registry information. Asset: 210, 208.
data_flows.description: Github API provides data (potentially via Github Proxy) to the Hyperlane Registry. (Doc 24, 45)

trust_boundaries.name: On-chain/Off-chain Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Separates smart contracts executing on blockchains from off-chain services and bots. Interactions typically involve message passing, transaction submission, and data indexing.

trust_boundaries.name: Inter-Blockchain Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Logical boundary separating different blockchain networks. Hyperlane components (Mailbox, TokenRouter, ICA, IQS etc.) exist on multiple chains, and messages/Relayers/Validators operate across this boundary. Validation is primarily handled by ISMs and Relayers.

trust_boundaries.name: Application-Protocol Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Interface between external users or applications and the core Hyperlane smart contracts and services. Validation includes smart contract access controls and function call rules.

trust_boundaries.name: Github API Integration Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Hyperlane Github Proxy Service and the external Github API. Validation relies on API key authentication.

trust_boundaries.name: GCP Secret Manager Integration Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between Hyperlane infrastructure (via GCP Service Account) and the GCP Secret Manager. Validation relies on GCP IAM and service account authentication.

behaviors.name: User Initiates Native Token Transfer
behaviors.description: A user calls the 'transferRemote' function on a NativeTokenRouter to send native assets (e.g., ETH) to another chain. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: TokenRouter Dispatches Message
behaviors.description: A TokenRouter contract calls the 'dispatch' function on the Mailbox contract to send an interchain message for a token transfer. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: Relayer Processes Message
behaviors.description: The Relayer off-chain service submits a 'process' transaction to the Mailbox contract on the destination chain to deliver a message. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: Mailbox Delivers to TokenRouter for Minting/Release
behaviors.description: The Mailbox on the destination chain calls 'handle' on the destination TokenRouter, which then mints synthetic tokens or releases collateral to the recipient. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: Developer Dispatches Custom Message
behaviors.description: A developer or application dispatches a generic interchain message through the Mailbox, potentially specifying custom hooks or metadata. (Doc 18)

behaviors.name: Mailbox Invokes Custom PostDispatchHook
behaviors.description: The Mailbox contract invokes the 'postDispatch' function on a developer-configured PostDispatchHook contract. (Doc 18)

behaviors.name: Mailbox Verifies Message with ISM
behaviors.description: The Mailbox contract on the destination chain invokes the 'verify' function on the recipient's configured Interchain Security Module (can be Custom, Multisig, etc.). (Doc 19)

behaviors.name: Mailbox Delivers Message to Recipient Application
behaviors.description: After successful ISM verification, the Mailbox delivers the message payload to the recipient application contract (e.g., HelloWorld Router). (Doc 19, 39)

behaviors.name: User Initiates Yield Asset Transfer
behaviors.description: A user calls 'transferRemote' on HypERC4626Collateral to transfer yield-bearing assets. (Doc 22)

behaviors.name: HypERC4626Collateral Deposits to Vault
behaviors.description: The HypERC4626Collateral contract deposits the underlying asset into an ERC4626 Vault. (Doc 22)

behaviors.name: Relayer Makes CCIP-read Request
behaviors.description: The Relayer sends a POST request to the CCIP-read server to fetch off-chain data or proofs. (Doc 29)

behaviors.name: Github Proxy Fetches Artifacts
behaviors.description: The Github Proxy service makes an authenticated API call to Github to retrieve contract artifacts or other repository data. (Doc 37)

behaviors.name: Scraper Stores Blockchain Data
behaviors.description: The Scraper agent writes indexed data from Hyperlane contracts into its Postgres database. (Doc 11)

behaviors.name: Validator Submits Attestation to ISM
behaviors.description: A Validator submits a signed attestation to an Interchain Security Module (e.g., Multisig ISM) to vouch for a message's validity. (Doc 10, 19)

behaviors.name: Deployer Executes Remote Calls via ICA
behaviors.description: A deployer account on the origin chain controls an Interchain Account on a remote chain to execute arbitrary calls. (Doc 20)

behaviors.name: User Deploys Core Contracts via CLI
behaviors.description: A user utilizes the Hyperlane CLI to deploy core Hyperlane contracts like the Mailbox. (Doc 31)

behaviors.name: User Deploys Warp Route via CLI
behaviors.description: A user utilizes the Hyperlane CLI to deploy Warp Route contracts (TokenRouters). (Doc 31)

behaviors.name: User Approves ERC20 for TokenRouter
behaviors.description: User approves a CollateralTokenRouter to spend their ERC20 tokens for a warp transfer. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: TokenRouter Pulls ERC20 Collateral
behaviors.description: A CollateralTokenRouter calls 'transferFrom' on an ERC20 contract to lock collateral. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: User Burns Synthetic Token for Transfer
behaviors.description: User initiates a transfer from a chain with synthetic tokens, causing the SyntheticTokenRouter to burn them. (Doc 21)

behaviors.name: User Initiates Interchain Query
behaviors.description: A user or developer submits a query through the Interchain Query System to a remote contract. (Doc 20)

behaviors.name: Developer/Agent Reads from Hyperlane Registry
behaviors.description: A developer or agent reads configuration data (addresses, metadata) from the Hyperlane Registry. (Doc 30, 45)
